design_name: Spur gear on a stepped shaft, standing upright along Z axis, centered at origin in XY. Three-section stepped shaft with integrated 20-tooth spur gear at middle section, through bore, and keyway slot.
prompt: A spur gear on a stepped shaft, standing upright along the Z axis, centered at the origin in XY. The shaft has three cylindrical sections from bottom to top: a 15mm diameter journal from Z=0 to Z=20, a 20mm diameter gear seat from Z=20 to Z=35, and a 15mm diameter journal from Z=35 to Z=55. A 20-tooth spur gear with a star-polygon tooth profile (40 vertices alternating between tip radius 22mm and root radius 18mm) is integrated at the gear seat section from Z=20 to Z=35 (15mm face width). An 8mm diameter bore runs through the full shaft length along Z. A keyway slot 3mm wide (Y) and 3mm deep (radially, from the bore surface at radius 4mm outward to radius 7mm) is cut through the gear seat section only (Z=20 to Z=35), centered on the +X side.
primitive_plan_summary: Spur gear on a stepped shaft, standing upright along Z axis, centered at origin in XY. Three-section stepped shaft with integrated 20-tooth spur gear at middle section, through bore, and keyway slot.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The model correctly implements all specified features: (1) Three-section stepped shaft along Z-axis with 15mm diameter journals (Z=0-20 and Z=35-55) and 20mm diameter gear seat (Z=20-35) — confirmed by bounding box zlen=55mm and visible stepped profile in all three views. (2) 20-tooth spur gear with star-polygon profile (40 vertices alternating between 22mm tip and 18mm root radii) at the gear seat section — confirmed by bounding box 44mm x 44mm (2×22mm tip radius) and the rendered views clearly showing the correct tooth profile with sharp alternating tips and roots. (3) 8mm diameter through-bore along the full shaft length — visible in the center (top-down) view showing the bore opening. (4) Keyway slot 3mm wide × 3mm deep from bore surface (R=4mm to R=7mm) in the gear seat section only (Z=20-35), centered on +X side — code correctly positions the box at X center=5.5mm. The volume of ~22,753 mm³ is physically plausible for this geometry. The model is valid, watertight, with no self-intersections. The center of mass slight X-offset (-0.033mm) is consistent with the asymmetric keyway cut on the +X side.
